model.label: GPT-5.2
model.description: OpenAI • Smaller GPT frontier model tuned for fast, cost-efficient reasoning and tool use.

pick.rationale: Arizona is an overwhelming favorite on every baseline indicator: both KenPom and Torvik rate the Wildcats #3 nationally with elite two-way efficiency, while LIU sits in the low-200s with a negative adjusted margin and a bottom-tier offense. Arizona also had no players listed on its most recent Big 12 availability report, reducing the main upset-risk variable. LIU’s only credible path is extreme three-point/turnover variance plus Arizona playing unusually sloppy, but the talent and efficiency gap is so large that Arizona should advance comfortably.

reasoningStep.title: Arizona’s top-3 efficiency profile vs LIU’s low-200s profile with no major availability flags
reasoningStep.summary: The matchup is a classic 1/16 quality gap: Arizona grades as an elite national-title caliber team on both offense and defense, while LIU profiles as a below-average tournament team that will struggle to score efficiently or get consistent stops.
evidence: Ratings: Arizona KenPom #3 (AdjO 127.2, AdjD 89.7, AdjEM +37.56) and Torvik #3 (AdjO 126.2, AdjD 91.1); LIU KenPom #216 (AdjO 105.7, AdjD 109.6, AdjEM -3.95) and Torvik #234 (AdjO 103.6, AdjD 109.7).

Availability/news: a March 13 Big 12 Tournament availability report listed Arizona with no players on the injury report, and no credible recent reporting surfaced indicating a key Arizona absence that would materially narrow the gap.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

result.text: Iowa State basketball vs Arizona final injury report released Skip to main content Home Sports Hawk Central Big Ten Sports Cyclone Insider High School Insider SportsbookWire Odds USA TODAY Sports Recruiting All Iowa Sports Awards Iowa State basketball vs Arizona Big 12 Tournament injury report Register staff report | Des Moines Register The final Big 12 availability report for Friday's 6 p.m. Big 12 Tournament semifinal game featuring Iowa State basketball vs Arizona was released 90 minutes before tip-off. The No. 5-seeded Cyclones have one player listed as out and another as a game-time decision. No surprises for Iowa State, as both players have held those designations for some time. Meanwhile, the 1-seed Wildcats have no players on the injury report. More: Where to watch Iowa State basketball today vs Arizona in Big 12 Tournament These two teams met once during the regular season, with Arizona winning 73-57 in Tucson on March 2. Here is the full availability report for Friday's Iowa State vs Arizona game. modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
